MacroNations

The dedicated LMS where economics is taught, played & assessed.

Interactive books, games, and tournaments. Simulate economies, run real regressions, grade derivations, AI-proof proctored exams — and a gradebook that understands the subject. Choose a course below.

About MacroNations

About MacroNations

Learn economics by playing it — one platform, many disciplines.

Season 2026 · Now Open

Govern an Economy.
Win the World.

MacroNations is the premier macroeconomic policy simulation tournament for university teams, independent players, and open competitors worldwide. Compete across 5 pillars, manage crises, and prove your team has what it takes to run a country.

See how it works →
🌍
Open Season — free for everyone
Every tournament is free this year. One global season, one playoff, one champion — with the course textbook open to all. The award is seeded by MacroNations.

Scoring System

Five Pillars of Economic Excellence

Each round you score across five dimensions of macroeconomic performance. Balance is key — neglecting any pillar costs you points.

📈

GDP Performance

Maximize economic growth through fiscal and monetary policy without overheating.

200 pts max
⚖️

Stability

Control inflation, manage debt, and maintain market confidence under pressure.

200 pts max
🤝

Equality

Reduce income inequality and ensure broad access to economic opportunity.

200 pts max
🌱

Sustainability

Transition to clean energy and responsible resource management for long-term health.

200 pts max
💡

Innovation

Drive R&D investment, technology adoption, and human capital development.

200 pts max
Play Your Way

Free for every classroom. Competitive for the world.

Your professor sets up a class tournament at no cost. Teams can also compete beyond their university can elect into the MacroNations Global Championship.

Free

Class Tournament

$0 per team

Your professor creates a private tournament for your course. Compete against classmates, earn a certificate, and climb your class leaderboard.

  • Full simulation — all 10 policy levers
  • Live class leaderboard
  • Crisis response rounds
  • Participation certificate for all
  • Top 8 enter playoff bracket
Global Championship

World Cup

$50 per team

Your professor elects your class tournament into the global season. Compete against top teams from universities worldwide for a shared award fund.

  • Everything in class tournament
  • Global cross-university rankings
  • Year-long season standings
  • Participation certificates for all · Winner certificate for champion
  • Cash awards — top finalists share the fund
  • 📖 Free textbook access — Applied Macroeconomics by Amarendra Sharma, PhD, both Principles & Intermediate levels
Open Season

Free for everyone. One global championship.

No entry fees, no tiers — every team competes on equal footing. Play your tournament, and every team that plays this year is seeded into one annual global championship that runs all the way to the finals.

Free · Everyone

Play & Compete

$0 per team

Join or create a team and run the full macroeconomic simulation. Whether you’re in a class tournament or playing independently, you’re automatically part of the global season.

  • Full simulation — all 10 policy levers
  • Live leaderboards & crisis rounds
  • Participation certificate for all
  • 📖 Free textbook access for everyone
  • Every team enters the annual global pool
Global Championship

One Season → Finals

Seeded by MacroNations

A single annual seeding gathers every team that played this year. The top teams advance through the playoff bracket to a year-end final — and the award is funded by MacroNations, not entry fees.

  • All teams seeded into one season pool
  • Top 8 advance to the playoff bracket
  • Quarterfinals → Semifinals → Final
  • Award seeded by MacroNations
  • Winner certificate for the champion
Global Championship

One award fund. Two teams share it.

The MacroNations World Cup Award Fund rewards the top finalists from both university tournaments and the Open Championship worldwide. It grows with every team that enters the global season. At the end of the year, the top finalists share it — distributed at management discretion per the Award Rules.

🥇
Winner
Majority share
🥈
Runner-up
Remainder share
🏫
Top 8
Playoff qualification

Seeded by MacroNations LLC. Supplemented by entry fees at management discretion. Minimum 20 paid teams required. Final amount confirmed at season close. Full terms in Award Rules.

Open to Everyone

Open Championship

Open year-round. Register and start any day from January 1 through November 20 — no fixed schedule. Once you submit your first round, you have 10 days to complete the simulation. The only hard deadline is November 30, the close of the season.

  • 1Register your team — up to 5 members, $50 entry fee per team
  • 2Submit Round 1 — once fee is confirmed, your 10-day clock starts on first submission
  • 3Complete 10 rounds — one every 24 hours, auto-advancing
  • 4Qualify for the World Cup — finish by Nov 30 to enter the playoff bracket
  • 📖Free textbook access — Applied Macroeconomics: From Theory to Policy Simulation, by Amarendra Sharma, PhD, at both Principles and Intermediate levels, included with your entry fee
Available year-round · Start any day Jan 1 – Nov 20 · Season closes Nov 30

How It Works

From Registration to Victory

Five simple steps from sign-up to competing on the global stage.

1

Register Your Team

Form a team of up to 5 players using any valid email. No affiliation required for the Open Championship. Professors selecting professor access need an institutional academic email (.edu, .ac.in, .ac.uk, .edu.au, etc.).

2

Set Your Policy Levers

Each round, adjust 10 macroeconomic levers — interest rates, fiscal spending, trade policy, and more.

3

Respond to Crises

Random economic events hit every team. How you respond determines your resilience score.

4

Climb the Leaderboard

Scores update after each round. The top 8 teams enter the playoff bracket and compete for a share of the competition award fund.

5

Win Real Awards

Top finalists share the competition award fund, seeded by MacroNations. Top 8 teams qualify for the playoff bracket. Certificates are issued to all participants.

Ready to Govern?

Join teams from around the world competing in the world's most rigorous macroeconomic policy simulation. Universities, independent players, and open competitors welcome.

Elect for Global Seasonal Tournament
If elected, your teams' performance will be linked to the paid MacroNations World Cup. Teams will see the payment link and their scores will count toward global rankings.
Contact [email protected] to get your tournament's Stripe payment link.

⚠️ Complete Tournament?

This action cannot be undone without a Reopen.

⚠️
This ends the tournament immediately
If you have rounds left to play, use Advance Round instead of Complete. Completing locks the final scores and triggers award fund confirmation.

Grant Fee Waiver

100% = full waiver. 50% = half-price.

Sign in or create your MacroNations account

Invalid email or password.

Forgot your password?

Use any valid email. Professors selecting the professor role need an institutional academic email (e.g. .edu, .ac.in, .ac.uk, .edu.au) to access professor features.
Researchers register as professors with an additional study-participation flag. You'll be reviewed by the Lead PI before gaining access to research features. Your professor account works normally in the meantime.
Minimum 8 characters. Your password is encrypted.
Please fill in all required fields.

By registering you agree to MacroNations Tournament Rules.

Take MacroNations for a Spin

A frictionless walkthrough that lets you experience MacroNations from both sides — as a professor setting up a class tournament, and as a student playing a round. Nothing here touches the real platform, your email, or any real tournament. Click around, break things, reset, repeat.
